testcase.name: @stynx/backend exports surface for audit > barrel surfaces at least one export
AuditInterceptor > passes through when no audit metadata is set on the handler
AuditInterceptor > writes an envelope with action/entity/entityId/tenantId/actorId/role
AuditInterceptor > falls back to the controller class name when entity is not set
AuditInterceptor > honors entityIdSelector and metadataSelector
AuditInterceptor > runs metadata through the redaction policy when configured
AuditInterceptor > omits entityId when inference yields nothing
AuditInterceptor > logs but does not fail the request when sink.write throws
AuditInterceptor > propagates handler errors
AuthContextGuard > throws Unauthorized when verifier returns no principal
AuthContextGuard > attaches principal + compatibility user/actor + tenantId on the request
AuthContextGuard > uses tenantResolver when configured and respects its result
AuthContextGuard > honors x-tenant-id header when no resolver is configured
AuthContextGuard > selects the single principal tenant when no header and resolver absent
AuthContextGuard > leaves tenantId undefined when principal has multiple tenants and no header/resolver
AuthContextGuard > throws Forbidden when tenantEntitlementPolicy denies the tenant
AuthContextGuard > passes through when tenantEntitlementPolicy approves
AuthContextGuard > honors a custom principalMapper when provided
AuthContextGuard > accepts array-form authorization header (passed through to verifier)
AuthContextGuard > preserves correlationId in principalContext when present
@stynx/backend exports surface for auth > barrel surfaces at least one export
@stynx/backend exports surface for authorization > barrel surfaces at least one export
backend SLA behavior > resolves URL categories and falls back to NONE
backend SLA behavior > samples requests, skips NONE categories, aggregates by window, and records errors
backend SLA behavior > wires SLA module defaults and logger sink warning branch
backend SLA behavior > describes branch: default SLA dependencies resolve URL and sample through logger sink
backend SLA behavior > describes branch: unknown SLA category uses fallback threshold and default percentile index
backend SLA behavior > describes branch: zero-sized SLA windows aggregate empty percentile histories
backend authorization and auth behavior > evaluates role and permission policies with all/any modes
backend authorization and auth behavior > guards authorization metadata with default and injected evaluators
backend authorization and auth behavior > describes branch: authorization default evaluator receives role-only and permission-only metadata
backend authorization and auth behavior > maps principals from request compatibility locations
backend authorization and auth behavior > describes branch: actor fallback preserves optional email and username
backend authorization and auth behavior > describes branch: actor fallback defaults malformed principal arrays
backend authorization and auth behavior > resolves tenant headers and claim-first entitlement branches
backend authorization and auth behavior > auth guard attaches principal context and resolves tenant paths
backend authorization and auth behavior > describes branch: auth guard accepts non-string array headers and omits blank resolver header
backend authorization and auth behavior > describes branch: auth guard extracts array tenant headers and omits undefined claims spread
backend module wiring branches > wires optional module providers and disabled platform pipeline features
backend module wiring branches > wires db-context variants and validates missing applier config
backend idempotency behavior > bypasses read methods and optional write keys
backend idempotency behavior > describes branch: header arrays without strings and missing methods bypass idempotency
backend idempotency behavior > rejects missing keys and strict non-durable writes
backend idempotency behavior > caches in-memory writes, replays them, and detects payload conflicts
backend idempotency behavior > waits on in-flight in-memory work for concurrent matching writes
backend idempotency behavior > describes branch: in-memory execution defaults status and path when response/url absent
backend idempotency behavior > describes branch: idempotency accepts array-form key headers
backend idempotency behavior > uses durable entries, reservations, races, fallbacks, and cleanup branches
backend idempotency behavior > clears durable reservations on handler errors and enforces strict race failure
backend idempotency behavior > covers durable direct replay, conflict, wait timeout, and cache cleanup
backend idempotency behavior > describes branch: durable replay/cache paths default status codes
backend idempotency behavior > describes branch: durable wait and fallback paths default missing status codes
backend idempotency behavior > describes branch: durable local replay returns before another lookup
backend rate-limit behavior > bypasses health checks and enforces in-memory buckets
backend rate-limit behavior > describes branch: rate-limit defaults request identity and constructor options
backend rate-limit behavior > resets expired buckets and prunes overflow entries
backend rate-limit behavior > uses distributed stores, cleanup, fallback, and strict failures
backend rate-limit behavior > covers in-memory bucket increment, cleanup no-op, cleanup error, and overflow deletion
backend rate-limit behavior > describes branch: distributed cleanup no-ops when store lacks cleanup method
backend audit and db context branch closure > describes branch: audit logs non-Error sink failures and omits optional principal fields
backend audit and db context branch closure > describes branch: audit redaction context includes present principal
backend audit and db context branch closure > describes branch: db context finalizer skips release when preparation never completed
backend audit and db context branch closure > describes branch: session applier drops blank list/scalar values
backend audit and db context branch closure > describes branch: tenant lifecycle tolerates missing headers object
@stynx/backend exports surface for common > barrel surfaces at least one export
@stynx/backend exports surface for db-context > barrel surfaces at least one export
DbContextInterceptor > skips applier when there is no principal on the request
DbContextInterceptor > skips applier when no client is resolvable
DbContextInterceptor > applies session context when principal + pgClient are present
DbContextInterceptor > uses a custom dbClientResolver when provided
DbContextInterceptor > acquires + releases via lifecycle when no client is on the request
DbContextInterceptor > continues without applying db context when lifecycle acquisition returns nothing
DbContextInterceptor > does not call release when no client was acquired by the interceptor
DbContextInterceptor > swallows release errors so they do not bubble out of the request
DbContextInterceptor > skips lifecycle acquire when no tenantId is on the request
getPrincipalFromRequest > returns request.principal when present
getPrincipalFromRequest > falls back to principalContext.principal
getPrincipalFromRequest > falls back to request.user
getPrincipalFromRequest > falls back to request.actor
getPrincipalFromRequest > returns undefined when no principal sources are present
getPrincipalFromRequest > treats non-array roles/permissions/tenants/claims on user as empty/defaults
backend decorators > attaches authorization and audit metadata
backend decorators > exports the current-principal decorator factory
backend decorators > resolves the current principal from request metadata
@stynx/backend exports surface for idempotency > barrel surfaces at least one export
@stynx/backend exports surface for identity-admin > barrel surfaces at least one export
IdentityAdminService > delegates list/get/update/disable/enable to the adapter
IdentityAdminService > delegates group / password / verify operations
IdentityAdminService > throws ServiceUnavailableException for getUserBySubject when adapter lacks support
IdentityAdminService > delegates getUserBySubject when the adapter supports it
IdentityAdminService > throws ServiceUnavailableException for setUserPassword when adapter lacks support
IdentityAdminService > throws ServiceUnavailableException for verifyUserChannels when adapter lacks support

IdentityAdminService > IdentityAdminError → HttpException mapping > IDENTITY_NOT_FOUND maps to %p class NotFoundException extends http_exception_1.HttpException { /** * Instantiate a `NotFoundException` Exception. * * @example * `throw new NotFoundException()` * * @usageNotes * The HTTP response status code will be 404. * - The `objectOrError` argument defines the JSON response body or the message string. * - The `descriptionOrOptions` argument contains either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause. * * By default, the JSON response body contains two properties: * - `statusCode`: this will be the value 404. * - `message`: the string `'Not Found'` by default; override this by supplying * a string in the `objectOrError` parameter. * * If the parameter `objectOrError` is a string, the response body will contain an * additional property, `error`, with a short description of the HTTP error. To override the * entire JSON response body, pass an object instead. Nest will serialize the object * and return it as the JSON response body. * * @param objectOrError string or object describing the error condition. * @param descriptionOrOptions either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause */ constructor(objectOrError, descriptionOrOptions = 'Not Found') { const { description, httpExceptionOptions } = http_exception_1.HttpException.extractDescriptionAndOptionsFrom(descriptionOrOptions); super(http_exception_1.HttpException.createBody(objectOrError, description, http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND), http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.NOT_FOUND, httpExceptionOptions); } }

IdentityAdminService > IdentityAdminError → HttpException mapping > IDENTITY_FORBIDDEN maps to %p class ForbiddenException extends http_exception_1.HttpException { /** * Instantiate a `ForbiddenException` Exception. * * @example * `throw new ForbiddenException()` * * @usageNotes * The HTTP response status code will be 403. * - The `objectOrError` argument defines the JSON response body or the message string. * - The `descriptionOrOptions` argument contains either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause. * * By default, the JSON response body contains two properties: * - `statusCode`: this will be the value 403. * - `message`: the string `'Forbidden'` by default; override this by supplying * a string in the `objectOrError` parameter. * * If the parameter `objectOrError` is a string, the response body will contain an * additional property, `error`, with a short description of the HTTP error. To override the * entire JSON response body, pass an object instead. Nest will serialize the object * and return it as the JSON response body. * * @param objectOrError string or object describing the error condition. * @param descriptionOrOptions either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause */ constructor(objectOrError, descriptionOrOptions = 'Forbidden') { const { description, httpExceptionOptions } = http_exception_1.HttpException.extractDescriptionAndOptionsFrom(descriptionOrOptions); super(http_exception_1.HttpException.createBody(objectOrError, description, http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN), http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN, httpExceptionOptions); } }

IdentityAdminService > IdentityAdminError → HttpException mapping > IDENTITY_CONFLICT maps to %p class ConflictException extends http_exception_1.HttpException { /** * Instantiate a `ConflictException` Exception. * * @example * `throw new ConflictException()` * * @usageNotes * The HTTP response status code will be 409. * - The `objectOrError` argument defines the JSON response body or the message string. * - The `descriptionOrOptions` argument contains either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause. * * By default, the JSON response body contains two properties: * - `statusCode`: this will be the value 409. * - `message`: the string `'Conflict'` by default; override this by supplying * a string in the `objectOrError` parameter. * * If the parameter `objectOrError` is a string, the response body will contain an * additional property, `error`, with a short description of the HTTP error. To override the * entire JSON response body, pass an object instead. Nest will serialize the object * and return it as the JSON response body. * * @param objectOrError string or object describing the error condition. * @param descriptionOrOptions either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause */ constructor(objectOrError, descriptionOrOptions = 'Conflict') { const { description, httpExceptionOptions } = http_exception_1.HttpException.extractDescriptionAndOptionsFrom(descriptionOrOptions); super(http_exception_1.HttpException.createBody(objectOrError, description, http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.CONFLICT), http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.CONFLICT, httpExceptionOptions); } }

IdentityAdminService > IdentityAdminError → HttpException mapping > IDENTITY_VALIDATION_ERROR maps to %p class BadRequestException extends http_exception_1.HttpException { /** * Instantiate a `BadRequestException` Exception. * * @example * `throw new BadRequestException()` * * @usageNotes * The HTTP response status code will be 400. * - The `objectOrError` argument defines the JSON response body or the message string. * - The `descriptionOrOptions` argument contains either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause. * * By default, the JSON response body contains two properties: * - `statusCode`: this will be the value 400. * - `message`: the string `'Bad Request'` by default; override this by supplying * a string in the `objectOrError` parameter. * * If the parameter `objectOrError` is a string, the response body will contain an * additional property, `error`, with a short description of the HTTP error. To override the * entire JSON response body, pass an object instead. Nest will serialize the object * and return it as the JSON response body. * * @param objectOrError string or object describing the error condition. * @param descriptionOrOptions either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause */ constructor(objectOrError, descriptionOrOptions = 'Bad Request') { const { description, httpExceptionOptions } = http_exception_1.HttpException.extractDescriptionAndOptionsFrom(descriptionOrOptions); super(http_exception_1.HttpException.createBody(objectOrError, description, http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST), http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, httpExceptionOptions); } }

IdentityAdminService > IdentityAdminError → HttpException mapping > IDENTITY_RATE_LIMITED maps to %p class ServiceUnavailableException extends http_exception_1.HttpException { /** * Instantiate a `ServiceUnavailableException` Exception. * * @example * `throw new ServiceUnavailableException()` * * @usageNotes * The HTTP response status code will be 503. * - The `objectOrError` argument defines the JSON response body or the message string. * - The `descriptionOrOptions` argument contains either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause. * * By default, the JSON response body contains two properties: * - `statusCode`: this will be the value 503. * - `message`: the string `'Service Unavailable'` by default; override this by supplying * a string in the `objectOrError` parameter. * * If the parameter `objectOrError` is a string, the response body will contain an * additional property, `error`, with a short description of the HTTP error. To override the * entire JSON response body, pass an object instead. Nest will serialize the object * and return it as the JSON response body. * * @param objectOrError string or object describing the error condition. * @param descriptionOrOptions either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause */ constructor(objectOrError, descriptionOrOptions = 'Service Unavailable') { const { description, httpExceptionOptions } = http_exception_1.HttpException.extractDescriptionAndOptionsFrom(descriptionOrOptions); super(http_exception_1.HttpException.createBody(objectOrError, description, http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE), http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, httpExceptionOptions); } }

IdentityAdminService > IdentityAdminError → HttpException mapping > IDENTITY_PROVIDER_ERROR maps to %p class ServiceUnavailableException extends http_exception_1.HttpException { /** * Instantiate a `ServiceUnavailableException` Exception. * * @example * `throw new ServiceUnavailableException()` * * @usageNotes * The HTTP response status code will be 503. * - The `objectOrError` argument defines the JSON response body or the message string. * - The `descriptionOrOptions` argument contains either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause. * * By default, the JSON response body contains two properties: * - `statusCode`: this will be the value 503. * - `message`: the string `'Service Unavailable'` by default; override this by supplying * a string in the `objectOrError` parameter. * * If the parameter `objectOrError` is a string, the response body will contain an * additional property, `error`, with a short description of the HTTP error. To override the * entire JSON response body, pass an object instead. Nest will serialize the object * and return it as the JSON response body. * * @param objectOrError string or object describing the error condition. * @param descriptionOrOptions either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause */ constructor(objectOrError, descriptionOrOptions = 'Service Unavailable') { const { description, httpExceptionOptions } = http_exception_1.HttpException.extractDescriptionAndOptionsFrom(descriptionOrOptions); super(http_exception_1.HttpException.createBody(objectOrError, description, http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE), http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, httpExceptionOptions); } }

IdentityAdminService > IdentityAdminError → HttpException mapping > UNKNOWN_CODE maps to %p class ServiceUnavailableException extends http_exception_1.HttpException { /** * Instantiate a `ServiceUnavailableException` Exception. * * @example * `throw new ServiceUnavailableException()` * * @usageNotes * The HTTP response status code will be 503. * - The `objectOrError` argument defines the JSON response body or the message string. * - The `descriptionOrOptions` argument contains either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause. * * By default, the JSON response body contains two properties: * - `statusCode`: this will be the value 503. * - `message`: the string `'Service Unavailable'` by default; override this by supplying * a string in the `objectOrError` parameter. * * If the parameter `objectOrError` is a string, the response body will contain an * additional property, `error`, with a short description of the HTTP error. To override the * entire JSON response body, pass an object instead. Nest will serialize the object * and return it as the JSON response body. * * @param objectOrError string or object describing the error condition. * @param descriptionOrOptions either a short description of the HTTP error or an options object used to provide an underlying error cause */ constructor(objectOrError, descriptionOrOptions = 'Service Unavailable') { const { description, httpExceptionOptions } = http_exception_1.HttpException.extractDescriptionAndOptionsFrom(descriptionOrOptions); super(http_exception_1.HttpException.createBody(objectOrError, description, http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE), http_status_enum_1.HttpStatus.SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, httpExceptionOptions); } }
IdentityAdminService > passes through non-IdentityAdminError throws unchanged
IdentityAdminService > local-sync adapter delegations > throws ServiceUnavailableException when no local-sync adapter is wired
IdentityAdminService > local-sync adapter delegations > delegates syncToLocal / syncUser / listGroupsWithMetaByUserId to the local-sync adapter
PormIdentityAdminFacade > list() expands each item via get() to include groups + attributes
PormIdentityAdminFacade > list() forwards every supported filter to listUsers
PormIdentityAdminFacade > list() handles items without a username inline
PormIdentityAdminFacade > describes branch: list() omits optional inline fields and next token when absent
PormIdentityAdminFacade > get() composes user details + group names
PormIdentityAdminFacade > describes branch: get() omits optional status and timestamp fields
PormIdentityAdminFacade > getBySub() resolves username via getUserBySubject then composes
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() short-circuits when no updatable attributes are set
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() short-circuits when custom is empty
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() passes through every present field to updateUser
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() treats email as independently updatable
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() treats phone as independently updatable
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() treats name as independently updatable
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() treats given name as independently updatable
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() treats family name as independently updatable
PormIdentityAdminFacade > update() treats custom as independently updatable
PormIdentityAdminFacade > disable/enable/addToGroup/removeFromGroup/resetPassword/setPassword return their tags
PormIdentityAdminFacade > verify() short-circuits when neither email nor phone is requested
PormIdentityAdminFacade > verify() delegates when email or phone is requested
PormIdentityAdminFacade > listGroups / listGroupsWithMetaByUserId / listAllGroups delegate
PormIdentityAdminFacade > describes branch: listAllGroups omits next token when provider has no token
PormIdentityAdminFacade > syncToLocal / syncUser delegate
PecIdentityAdminFacade > list() maps items into PEC summary shape with parsed dates
PecIdentityAdminFacade > list() emits null email/phone when adapter returns undefined
PecIdentityAdminFacade > get() maps user detail with parsed dates
PecIdentityAdminFacade > update() filters undefined fields, calls updateUser, returns mapped detail
PecIdentityAdminFacade > describes branch: PEC update passes an empty patch when all fields are undefined
PecIdentityAdminFacade > disable/enable/listGroups/addToGroup/removeFromGroup/verify/resetPassword delegate
PecIdentityAdminFacade > listAllGroups() maps items + nextToken
PecIdentityAdminFacade > describes branch: PEC listAllGroups omits next token when absent
backend durable stores > covers backend PgIdempotencyStore query branches
backend durable stores > describes branch: PgIdempotencyStore handles sparse rows and array row counts
backend durable stores > covers backend PgRateLimitStore query branches
backend durable stores > describes branch: PgRateLimitStore uses default SQL identifiers
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > syncToLocal skips users without a username or UUID subject
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > describes branch: syncToLocal skips users whose subject is missing entirely
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > syncToLocal aggregates group memberships and upserts roles
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > describes branch: syncToLocal persists optional status when provider supplies it
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > syncToLocal paginates listUsers + listGroups via nextToken
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > syncUser throws IDENTITY_VALIDATION_ERROR when sub is not a UUID
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > syncUser returns the resolved user id
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > describes branch: syncUser omits optional status and email when absent
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > listGroupsWithMetaByUserId throws IDENTITY_NOT_FOUND when no username row exists
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > listGroupsWithMetaByUserId merges adapter groups with isIn flag and meta sort
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > listGroupsWithMetaByUserId handles missing meta loader (default empty)
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > falls back to direct run() when db.withTransaction is not present
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > honors custom roleCodeFromGroupName and roleDescriptionFromGroup
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > default role descriptions normalize group names when descriptions are blank
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > honors custom userIdFromDetail override (e.g. external_id strategy)
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > honors custom displayNameFromDetail override
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > default display name falls back through name → custom:displayName → email → username
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > allows default display-name resolution to return undefined when all candidates are blank
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > sorts groups by name when sort order ties or is absent
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > default role-code strategy strips trailing s from plural group names
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > describes branch: missing returned role ids skip group and membership counts
PgIdentityLocalSyncAdapter > rejects UUID-looking subjects only when the full string matches
loadPormRoleMetaRows > queries the PORM enum view + maps rows to IdentityGroupMetaRow
loadPormRoleMetaRows > honors viewName and roleDomain overrides + falls back to sort_order when order missing
loadPormRoleMetaRows > describes branch: loadPormRoleMetaRows accepts array results and null sort order
PgSessionDbContextApplier > issues SET row_security = on by default
PgSessionDbContextApplier > skips SET row_security when enableRowSecurity=false
PgSessionDbContextApplier > writes tenantId via set_config to all default tenant setting names
PgSessionDbContextApplier > writes userId, correlationId, requestId via set_config
PgSessionDbContextApplier > joins roles and permissions arrays into comma-separated strings
PgSessionDbContextApplier > clears settings to empty string when value is undefined and clearMissing=true (default)
PgSessionDbContextApplier > skips set_config for absent values when clearMissing=false
PgSessionDbContextApplier > honors custom setting name overrides and deduplicates
PgSessionDbContextApplier > resolves language from extras["lang"] over defaultLanguage
PgSessionDbContextApplier > falls back to defaultLanguage when no extras key matches
PgSessionDbContextApplier > reads orgCnpj from extras["orgCnpj"] or ["org_cnpj"]
PgSessionDbContextApplier > honors custom languageExtraKeys ordering
PgSessionDbContextApplier > skips applying when settings list is empty (zero-length override)
PgSessionDbContextApplier > coerces non-string scalar values to strings via String()
@stynx/backend exports surface for pipeline > barrel surfaces at least one export
@stynx/backend exports surface for rate-limit > barrel surfaces at least one export
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > returns undefined when metadata is undefined
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > returns the value unchanged for trivial scalar values inside objects
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > redacts values under sensitive keys (default pattern)
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > honors custom sensitiveKeyPattern + replacement
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > truncates long strings to maxStringLength with ellipsis
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > truncates large arrays to maxArrayLength
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > serializes Date values as ISO strings
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > emits [MaxDepth] beyond maxDepth
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > returns undefined when metadata sanitizes to an array (root-level array)
PatternAuditMetadataRedactionPolicy > emits [Unsupported] for function/symbol values
PgTenantDbClientLifecycle > acquire delegates to factory.connectWithTenant with the tenantId
PgTenantDbClientLifecycle > release calls client.release() by default
PgTenantDbClientLifecycle > release honors releaseMethodName override (e.g. "close")
PgTenantDbClientLifecycle > release is a no-op when the client does not expose the release method
ResponseEventRequestDbClientLifecycle > acquire delegates straight through
ResponseEventRequestDbClientLifecycle > release defers to the response finish event when response is open
ResponseEventRequestDbClientLifecycle > releases immediately when the response is already finished
ResponseEventRequestDbClientLifecycle > releases immediately when there is no response to listen on
ResponseEventRequestDbClientLifecycle > does not double-release when both finish and close fire
ResponseEventRequestDbClientLifecycle > resolves response from request.res when request.response is absent
ResponseEventRequestDbClientLifecycle > skips the second release call when invoked twice for the same request
ResponseEventRequestDbClientLifecycle > honors custom releaseEvents config
@stynx/backend exports surface for sla > barrel surfaces at least one export
@stynx/backend exports surface for storage > barrel surfaces at least one export
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > grants when a string claim contains the tenantId
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > grants when an array claim contains the tenantId
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > grants via JSON-array string claim
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > grants via CSV string claim
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > denies when a tenant claim is present but does not include the tenantId
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > denies when no tenant claim exists and no fallback configured
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > delegates to fallback when no tenant claim and fallback configured
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > does NOT delegate to fallback when a tenant claim exists but excludes the tenantId
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > falls back through invalid JSON to CSV parsing
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > ignores non-string and blank string claims
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > ignores non-string entries in array claims
ClaimFirstTenantEntitlementPolicy > honors custom claimKeys list
SqlTenantEntitlementFallback > returns true when a row matches subject + tenant
SqlTenantEntitlementFallback > returns false when no rows match
SqlTenantEntitlementFallback > returns false when both subject and email are missing
SqlTenantEntitlementFallback > omits active predicate when activeColumn is null
SqlTenantEntitlementFallback > rejects table identifiers that fail the SQL identifier pattern
SqlTenantEntitlementFallback > handles array-form query result (no .rows wrapper)
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > throws BadRequestException when tenant header missing and required
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > allows missing header when requireTenantHeader=false
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > throws when header present but not a UUID and enforceTenantUuid=true
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > accepts a non-UUID header when enforceTenantUuid=false
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > binds request.tenantId from the valid UUID header
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > reads from the array form of the header (takes first element)
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > falls back to the lowercased variant of the configured header name
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > releases the pgClient on response finish
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > does not double-release on finish + close
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > falls back to dbClient when pgClient is absent
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > is a no-op when no client is attached to the request
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > skips release bindings when response is not an EventEmitter
TenantLifecycleMiddleware > createTenantLifecycleMiddleware returns a bound function form
